content.claim: Finland's PISA mathematics score fell 64 points from 548 (2003) to 484 (2022), and achievement inequality has increased in recent cycles with growing SES achievement gaps despite the formal absence of tracking.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Declining scores reflect multiple potential causes (curriculum changes, teacher workforce, pandemic effects, measurement factors) not isolated by PISA data alone
SES gap increase is documented trend but mechanism—whether driven by informal selection, residential segregation, or external economic factors—is not uniquely identified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
